title: Coverage, Thresholds, and Meaningful Evidence · Policy Clinic: Threshold Design
summary: Set coverage policy that exposes risk without encouraging empty tests. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates threshold design increment in a changed-code coverage and exception policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose global, package, and changed-code thresholds from risk and baseline.
Interpret line, branch, function, condition, and path coverage limitations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Build Threshold Design evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-threshold-design-qqx775: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Threshold Design into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-threshold-design-qqx775:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Threshold Design structural check is only one gate. a changed-code coverage and exception policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose global, package, and changed-code thresholds from risk and baseline.
